Title: Front-Line Mortgage Rate Retail Chatbot
Product Description: A conversational LLM application deployed on a bank’s public mobile app. It answers basic user questions about mortgage interest rates, branch hours, and documentation requirements. It does not perform underwriting or log customer financial decisions.
Expected Role: Provider (White-labeling a basic API but offering it under their trademark).
S1 (Purpose/Domain): 100% (Informational client services; explicitly excludes credit risk evaluation).
S2 (Supply Chain): 80% (Calls external foundational model but wraps it locally).
S3 (Autonomy): 100% (Outputs conversations directly to retail users in real time).
S5 (Integration): 30% (Isolated web frontend, no backend ledger hooks).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(1): Providers must ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed so users are explicitly informed they are interacting with AI.

Title: Synthetic Document Generator for KYC Stress-Testing
Product Description: A FinTech platform utilizes generative adversarial models (GANs) to manufacture thousands of highly realistic, completely fake UK passports and utility bills to stress-test their automated Know-Your-Customer (KYC) image processing software.
Expected Role: Provider (Generating synthetic media for internal software validation).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(2): AI systems that generate synthetic audio, image, video, or text content must ensure outputs are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated.

Title: Generative Personal Finance Blog Copywriter
Product Description: A wealth management firm uses an LLM API to write weekly educational articles on "Understanding ISA Tax Allowances" which are published directly to their public-facing corporate blog to drive traffic.
Expected Role: Deployer (Operating an external generative system to publish content).
S3 (Autonomy): 50% (AI writes drafts, marketing team copyedits before publishing).
S4 (Interface/Modality): 100% (Text generation published to the public web).
S5 (Integration): 10% (Connected only to a content management system like WordPress).
Notes: Triggers Article 50(4): Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text published to inform the public on matters of public interest must disclose that the text has been artificially generated.

Title: Automated Personalized Account Summary Generator
Product Description: An app that runs an LLM to take raw monthly transaction lists and summarize them into a friendly, narrative email statement (e.g., "Looks like you spent a bit more on artisan coffee in Shoreditch this month!") sent directly to the customer.
Expected Role: Provider (Packaging the presentation feature within their financial application).
Notes: Requires disclosure under Article 50(1) and 50(2) because it generates synthetic text tailored directly to individuals.

Title: Inbound Support Email Sentiment Classifier
Product Description: A tool that reads incoming customer service emails to classify whether the tone is "Angry," "Neutral," or "Satisfied" purely to route priority cases to senior staff faster. It has no impact on contract terms, credit status, or employee tracking.
Expected Role: Provider (Built and integrated into internal CRM).
Notes: Categorized as an emotion classification system under Article 50(3). The deployer must inform the natural persons exposed to the system of its operation, provided it does not cross into prohibited workplace monitoring fields.

Title: Synthetic Customer Explainer Avatars
Product Description: A FinTech platform generates digital video avatars to explain personal pension restructuring options to users instead of recording live human actors.
Expected Role: Deployer (Using a synthetic video generation software provider).
Notes: Triggers strict deepfake/synthetic media transparency rules under Article 50(2); the video must prominently display an indicator that the avatar is artificial.

Title: Synthetic Market Tick-Data Generator
Product Description: An algo-trading startup uses deep generative modeling to create artificial stock market transaction ticks to test their trading execution latency speeds.
Expected Role: Provider (Created the generator for engineering data runs).
Notes: Requires machine-readable metadata marking under Article 50(2) to guarantee that synthetic database assets are never accidentally mixed into live production or market reporting streams.
